title: selinum pollichii
intro: Selinum pollichii, also known as Pollich's wild carrot or Queen anne's lace, is a perennial plant in the carrot family native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive problems, as well as being used as a diuretic. Selinum pollichii can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, making it a versatile remedy in folk medicine.
